coredata.dc:description: In this paper, novel copper-doped sulfur-rich carbon dots (Cu, S-CDs) nanozymes with high peroxidase-like (POD-like) activity were fabricated, and an innovative fluorescent system for NO2 - detection was further developed based on dopamine (DA)-mediated oxidation pathway as well as nanozymes. Specifically, an azocine derivative with strong green fluorescence was generated by the reaction of DA and 5, 7-dihydroxy-4-methyl-2h-benzopyrane-2-one (DMB) under the catalysis of Cu, S-CDs with peroxidase-like activity, which was susceptible to the sensitive NO2 - analysis. In the presence of NO2 -, it underwent with DA to form a non-fluorescent product of 4-ethylcatechol, and prevented the formation of azocine derivative and quenched the fluorescence of system. Based on the comparative difference of fluorescence signals of the detection system, the NO2 - detection could be readily achieved. Also, the NO2 - detecting platform had been shown efficiency and satisfactory results in various real samples such as tap water, sausage and pickles.

head.ce:title: Dopamine-mediated strategy coupled with the nanozyme catalysis of copper-doped sulfur-rich CDs for detecting NO2 - in differential fluorescence

ce:affiliation.ce:label: a
ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: School of Chemistry and Biological Engineering, University of Science and Technology Beijing, Beijing 100083, China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: In this paper, novel copper-doped sulfur-rich carbon dots (Cu, S-CDs) nanozymes with high peroxidase-like (POD-like) activity were fabricated, and an innovative fluorescent system for NO2 - detection was further developed based on dopamine (DA)-mediated oxidation pathway as well as nanozymes. Specifically, an azocine derivative with strong green fluorescence was generated by the reaction of DA and 5, 7-dihydroxy-4-methyl-2h-benzopyrane-2-one (DMB) under the catalysis of Cu, S-CDs with peroxidase-like activity, which was susceptible to the sensitive NO2 - analysis. In the presence of NO2 -, it underwent with DA to form a non-fluorescent product of 4-ethylcatechol, and prevented the formation of azocine derivative and quenched the fluorescence of system. Based on the comparative difference of fluorescence signals of the detection system, the NO2 - detection could be readily achieved. Also, the NO2 - detecting platform had been shown efficiency and satisfactory results in various real samples such as tap water, sausage and pickles.

ce:data-availability.ce:para: The data that has been used is confidential.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Introduction
ce:section.ce:para: Nanozymes, as a class of enzyme mimics, not only has the unique properties of nanomaterials, but also has excellent catalytic efficiency. Compared with natural enzymes, it has the advantages of low cost, easy large-scale production, resistance to harsh environment, long-term storage, etc., so it has attracted much attention [1]. At present, a variety of nanozyme materials have been synthesized, including metal-organic framework materials [2], metallic oxide [3], carbon dots (CDs) [4] and monatomic materials [5], etc., and have been widely employed for sensing [6], biological imaging [7], medical therapy [8] and photocatalysis [9]. Among them, CDs nanozymes have attracted much attention due to their high stability, adjustable optical properties, non-toxicity, good biocompatibility, easy synthesis and functionalization [10]. In addition, the heteroatoms or transition metal elements could be doped to further improve enzyme-like activity of CDs [11]. Especially, Cu-doped CDs nanozymes have been widely studied because of their low price, high catalytic activity and good stability [12,13].

NO2 - is a form of natural nitrogen that plays an important role in ecosystems [14]. It is also widely applied in the food processing industry as a food additive and preservative to enhance characteristic color, improve flavor, and inhibit bacterial growth [15]. However, excessive intake of NO2 - can even lead to hypoxia of human tissues, methemoglobin disease, and gastric carcinoma [16]. So the maximum daily intake of NO2 - in drinking water and food were 3 mg/L (65.2 μM) and 0.06 mg/kg/day respectively, established by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the Joint Food and Agriculture Organization/World Health Organization Expert Committee on Food Additives [17]. Therefore, rapid and sensitive determination of NO2 - in the environment and food is of great significance. At present, various methods have been developed for assessment of NO2 -, such as electrochemical method [18], spectrophotometry [19], chemiluminescence method [20] and polarography [21]. In recent years, with the rapid development of nanozyme field, many unique colorimetric sensors for NO2 - have emerged [22–29]. Usually, 3, 3’, 5, 5’-tetramethylbenzidine (TMB) was typically employed as chromogenic substrate in NO2 - colorimetric methods based on nanozymes. The active free radicals produced by the nanozymes could catalyze the oxidation of colorless TMB to dark blue oxidized TMB (oxTMB), and when oxTMB selectively reacted with NO2 -, it would be formed diazotized oxTMB and show out the color changes of blue to green and then green to yellow. Meanwhile, fluorescence spectroscopy had been widely concerned because of its simplicity, high sensitivity, desirable selectivity and low limit of detection (LOD). However, literatures downplayed reports on the developing fluorescent substrates suitable for nanozyme systems in NO2 - detection.

Pictet-Spengler reaction is a cyclocondensation reaction of β-arylethylamine with aldehydes or ketones catalyzed by acid, which is often used to synthesize tetrahydroisoquinoline and β-carboline derivatives [30,31]. In 2013, following this reaction mechanism, Acuña synthesized fluorescent oxocines and azocines for the first time (Fig. S1), and further discovered that they had good fluorescence quantum yield (QY) and biocompatibility, which had attracted people's attention [32]. Subsequently, it was also found that the interaction between DA and phenolic substances could form the azocine derivatives under alkaline conditions and strong oxidizing conditions (derived from highly oxidizing substances or produced under the catalysis of nano-enzymes or natural enzymes) based on the Michael addition reaction and Pictet-Spengler reaction [33,34], which had been employed for fluorescent detection of tyrosinase activity [33], resorcinol [35], alkaline phosphatase activity [36,37], cardiac troponin [38,39] and DA [33,40–42]. And phenolic reactive substances had also been explored to some extent, such as resorcinol [33–36], 3, 5-dihydroxytoluene [39], 3, 5-dihydroxybenzoic acid [39], β-naphthol [40], 1, 3-dihydroxynaphthalene [37], 1, 5-dihydroxynaphthalene [38], and 3-diethylaminophenol (Fig. S2) [42]. Nevertheless, it was regrettable that there have been no reports on detecting NO2 - via the formation of strong fluorescence oxocine or azocine derivatives, not to mention the achievement or generation of heterocyclic structures for detecting NO2 - with long wavelength fluorescence emission and high QY by Michael addition reaction and Pictet-Spengler reaction up to now.

Herein, we designed and generated nanozyme employing POD-like catalytic activity (Cu, S-CDs), while, coumarin-like phenolic derivative (DMB) was employed as reactants to provide specific fluorescent substances through fluorogenic reaction with DA, and NO2 - was indirectly recognized and detected across the comparative difference of fluorescent intensity ( Scheme 1). From this strategy, DA-mediated reaction coupled with the catalysis of Cu, S-CDs was carried out for detecting NO2 - in fluorescence.

In brief, with DA and DMB as substrates, Michael addition reaction and Pictet-Spengler reaction were performed under the catalysis of Cu, S-CDs to obtain high QY azocine derivative with green fluorescence. On the other hand, DA would be consumed by the existing NO2 - to form 4-ethyl catechol based on deamination reaction, which reduced or interrupted the formation of azocine derivative from DA and DMB. The indirect recognition and quantitative analysis of NO2 - would be realized by the difference of fluorescent intensity at 494 nm. The specific interaction between DA and NO2 - ensured the good selectivity of the system. Meanwhile, the desirable catalysis of Cu, S-CDs nanozymes and the specific fluorogenic reaction between DMB and DA made this fluorescence method achieve high sensitivity and accuracy toward NO2 -.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Materials and methods
Reagents and instruments
ce:section.ce:para: Reagents and instruments were provided in Supporting Information.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Preparation of Cu, S-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: 2, 2′-dithiobenzoic acid (DSTA, 136 mg), copper chloride (0.2 g) and acetic acid (10 mL) were added into 25 mL beaker, and ultrasonic was carried out for 20 min to disperse the powder uniformly in the solvent. The mixed suspension was then transferred to a 25 mL reactor and reacted at 180°C for 12 h. Upon the completion of the reaction, the reactor was cooled to room temperature, and the prepared CDs solution was added to the hot deionized water (100 mL) to stand for 15 min, then filtered, and washed with deionized water and ethanol to wash away the residual substance. The washed sediment was dried at 80℃ for 5 h to obtain dry Cu, S-CDs powder.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Enzyme-like activities characterization of Cu, S-CDs
ce:section.ce:para:
To assess the POD-like activities of Cu, S-CDs by using TMB as chromogenic substrate, Cu, S-CDs (10 mg/mL, 40 µL) was mixed in the HAc-NaAc buffer solution (9380 µL) at pH= 5.0, followed by the addition of TMB (80 µL) at different concentrations and H2O2 (500 µL) at different concentrations. Afterward, the solution mixture was incubated at 30℃ for 20 min. Meanwhile, the absorbance change of the solution was recorded by ultraviolet spectrophotometer, and the steady-state kinetic analysis of the POD-like activities of Cu, S-CDs was conducted. Ultimately, Michaelis-Menten parameters, maximum reaction rate (Vmax) and Michaelis constant (Km), were calculated according to the Lineweaver-Burk figure, as shown below.

In the formula, V and Vmax represented the initial reaction rate and the maximum reaction rate respectively, [S] corresponded to the substrate concentration, and Km was Michaelis constant.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Determination of fluorescence products from Pictet-Spengler reaction catalyzed by Cu, S-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: Cu, S-CDs (10 mg/mL, 40 µL) were introduced to HAc-NaAc buffer solution (9360 µL, pH=5.0), followed by DA (30 mM, 50 µL), DMB (30 mM, 50 µL), and H2O2 (25 mM, 500 µL). After that, the solution mixture was incubated at 30℃, and the fluorescence spectrum of the system was recorded. Finally, the concentration of DA (0–150 μM) was also titrated in the best performance.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Synthesis of response products of NO2 - and DA
ce:section.ce:para: Above all, NO2 - aqueous solution (75 mM, 10 mL) was slowly added drop by drop to a mixture of DA aqueous solution (5 mM, 70 mL, pH=2.0) and EtOH (20 mL). The mixture was then stirred thoroughly for 1 h to ensure that the reaction was complete. After the reaction was over, adjust the solution to neutral and remove the solvent under reduced pressure. Finally, the solid product was purified by column chromatography. The eluents were ethyl acetate (EA) and petroleum ether (PE) (1:10), and the white product 4-ethylcatechol was obtained.

ce:section.ce:section-title: DA-mediated NO2 - fluorescence assay catalyzed by Cu, S-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: Under the trigger of H2O2 and Cu, S-CDs, DA was used as the mediator to construct the fluorescent system for detection of NO2 -. The operation was performed as follows: After adding DA solution (30 mM, 50 µL) and 2 mL of anhydrous ethanol to dilute hydrochloric acid solution (7350 µL, pH=2.0) and mixed well, then, different concentrations of NO2 - (10 μL) were added to the above mixed solution and reacted at 30℃ for 5 min. Subsequently, the pH of the solution was adjusted to 5.0, and Cu, S-CDs (10 mg/mL, 40 µL), DMB (30 mM, 50 µL), and H2O2 (25 mM, 500 µL) were added and incubated for 22 min. Finally, the fluorescence emission intensity of the solution was measured in the range of 460–650 nm (λex=450 nm). By comparing the fluorescence intensity difference between the system with and without NO2 -, the fluorescence quantitative detection of NO2 - could be realized.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Selectivity and anti-interference tests for detection of NO2 - by Cu, S-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: In the selectivity experiment, the concentration of all interfering substances, including F-, Cl-, Br-, I-, SO4 2-, CO3 2-, NO3 -, H2PO4 -, ClO-, MnO4 -, Na+, K+, Ca2+, Mg2+, Zn2+ and Al3+, phenylalanine (Phe), glutamate (Glu), glycine (Gly), tyrosine (Tyr), vitamin C (Vc), glutathione (GSH) and 1-iodobutane, was the same as that of NO2 -. At the same time, in order to test the anti-interference ability of the constructed sensor system for NO2 -, the influence of these possible co-existing substances in food or environment were investigated. The specific procedure was the same as the method for NO2 - response (λex=450 nm, λem=494 nm).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Analysis of NO2 - in actual samples
ce:section.ce:para: Tap water, sausages and pickles bought from supermarket were selected as actual samples. The tap water was processed through a 0.22 μm filter and the filtrate was collected for further testing. Then, sausage (5 g) or pickles (5 g) were poured to saturated borax solution (50 g/L, 12.5 mL) and boiling ultra-pure water (150 mL), soaked for 15 min and cooled down to room temperature. Subsequently, potassium ferrocyanide solution (106 g/L, 5 mL) and zinc acetate solution (220 g/L, 5 mL) were added and shaken for 30 min, and finally the supernatant was extracted by high-speed centrifugation and determined. In the meantime, different concentrations of NO2 - standard solutions (1, 30, 70 μM) were added to the actual sample solution for determination.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Results and discussion
Structural characterization of Cu, S-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: Encouraged by catalytic active activity of transition metal in nanozyme, Cu, S-CDs with POD-like catalytic activity were designed and synthesized based on Cu-S interaction. Cu, S-CDs were prepared by solvothermal method with DSTA and CuCl2 ( Fig. 1a), and their structures and properties were characterized by TEM, XRD, FTIR, UV–vis and XPS. As displayed in Fig. 1b, it could be clearly observed from TEM images that the distribution of Cu, S-CDs was very uniform, whose average particle size was about 2.05 nm. High-resolution TEM (HR-TEM) revealed that Cu, S-CDs had obvious lattice fringes with lattice spacing of about 0.21 nm, corresponding to the (100) plane of graphitic carbon [43]. In addition, the XRD pattern of Cu, S-CDs was shown in Fig. 1c, from which the characteristic diffraction peaks of Cu2O, CuO and Cu2S could be clearly observed, and the graphite-like structure was also shown [44].

Subsequently, the functional group composition of Cu, S-CDs was further studied by FTIR. As displayed in Fig. 1d, the surface of Cu, S-CDs mainly contained S-H (2567 cm−1), CO (1687 cm−1), CC (1562, 1460 cm−1), C-H (1419, 1152, 898, 647, 550 cm−1), C-C (1257 cm−1), C-O (1036 cm−1), C-S (739 cm−1), S-S (491 cm−1) [45].

The UV–vis spectra of Cu, S-CDs exhibited strong absorption in the UV region of 250–400 nm (Fig. 1e), while weak absorption in the visible region, which conformed to the UV absorption peak characteristics of CDs. Cu, S-CDs had a slight acromion at 335 nm in the UV–vis spectrum, which may be caused by electron transitions from C-O or CO bonds to π* orbitals [12]. Moreover, it could be seen from Fig. S3a that Cu, S-CDs exhibited fluorescence at 482 nm with an excitation wavelength of 410 nm. Then, an assessment was made on the stability of Cu, S-CDs. As presented in Fig. S3b, there was a slight decrease in the emission intensity of Cu, S-CDs after being stored in the solution for 7 days, suggesting that it possessed good stability.

In order to further study the structure of Cu, S-CDs, the chemical structure of CDs was also analyzed by XPS. The high-resolution XPS spectrum O 1 s consisted of two parts, 532.3 eV and 530.7 eV, corresponding to C-O-C/C-OH and CO, respectively (Fig. 1f). The high-resolution XPS spectrum of the S 2p band was divided into three peaks of 167.4 eV, 162.9 eV and 161.8 eV (Fig. 1g), which were attributed to the presence of C-(SO)x-C, CS and C-S, respectively [46]. Cu 2p spectra revealed four peaks at 953.8 eV, 951.4 eV, 933.9 eV and 931.5 eV respectively, corresponding to the presence of Cu2+ 2p1/2, Cu+ 2p1/2, Cu2+ 2p3/2 and Cu+ 2p3/2, which indicated successful doping of Cu elements (Fig. 1h) [47]. In summary, Cu, S-CDs were prepared successfully.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Enzyme-like activity study of Cu, S-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: As shown in Fig. 2a, by comparing the four groups of experiments, there were no significant UV–vis absorption in Cu, S-CDs, H2O2+TMB and Cu, S-CDs+TMB. While, it could be observed that only when Cu, S-CDs interacted with H2O2 and TMB, the solution of Cu, S-CDs+H2O2+TMB had obvious ultraviolet absorption in the range of 500–750 nm, and the color changed from colorless to blue, indicating that Cu, S-CDs had POD-like activity. In order to prepare and obtain the best POD-like activity of Cu, S-CDs, the ratio of Cu2+ and DSTA was optimized in the preparation process. The experimental results revealed that when the ratio (Cu2+/DSTA) = 3, the enzyme-like activities of Cu, S-CDs reached the highest level (Fig. 2b).

Subsequently, the effects of pH and temperature on the POD-like activity of Cu, S-CDs were further explored, and the POD-like activities of Cu, S-CDs were compared with those of horseradish peroxidase (HRP) in the same parameter range. It could be found from Fig. 2c and Fig. 2d that Cu, S-CDs exhibited the best POD-like activity under the temperature of 30℃ and pH of 5.0. Compared with HRP, Cu, S-CDs had wider application range of temperature and pH. When the reaction time was 15 min, the catalytic activity reached its peak (Fig. 2e). Fig. S3c revealed that no marked difference in the POD-mimicking catalytic ability of Cu, S-CDs was observed for 7 days and demonstrated the good stability. Finally, the specific activity (SA) of Cu, S-CDs as peroxidase was calculated to be 0.13 U/mg by converting the mass of it into enzyme activity units (Fig. 2f). Based on the above experimental results, the optimal enzyme-like conditions of Cu, S-CDs with TMB as chromogenic substrate were determined as follows: Cu, S-CDs (0.04 mg/mL), TMB (0.2 mM) and H2O2 (1.25 mM) were incubated at 30℃ and pH= 5.0 for 15 min.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Steady-state kinetic assay of Cu, S-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: Vmax and Km were analyzed by monitoring the POD-like catalytic performance of Cu, S-CDs at different concentrations of TMB and H2O2. As presented in Fig. 3, the catalytic curves of Cu, S-CDs on TMB and H2O2 were hyperbolic, which accorded with the Michaelis-Menten equation [48]. Next, the double reciprocal diagram was obtained by fitting the linear relationship between 1/V and the concentration of 1/TMB or 1/H2O2. The Km values of Cu, S-CDs for TMB and H2O2 were 0.0342 mM and 0.639 mM respectively, which were lower than HPR, demonstrating that Cu, S-CDs had better affinity for TMB and H2O2. At the same time, Vmax for TMB and H2O2 were 2.13 × 10−8 M/s and 6.77 × 10−8 M/s, respectively, manifesting that Cu, S-CDs had fast catalytic decomposition rates for H2O2 and good catalytic activity for TMB.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Mechanism of enzyme-like activity of Cu, S-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: To understand the POD-like activity of Cu, S-CDs, electron paramagnetic resonance (EPR) tests were performed. Among it, 5, 5-dimethyl-1-pyrroline N-oxide (DMPO) was selected as a free radical catcher and deionized water as solvent to capture •OH in the reaction process. DMPO could combine with •OH to generate the free radical adduct DMPO-OH (Fig. S4). The detection results displayed that the EPR spectral intensity ratio was 1:2:2:1, which accorded with the spectral characteristics of •OH captured by DMPO ( Fig. 4a), and proved the generation of •OH in the system with Cu, S-CDs and H2O2 [49].

Furthermore, the other metal-doped CDs (with Cu2+, Zn2+, Fe2+, Ir3+, Mn2+, Ni2+, Co2+, Ce4+, Ce3+ and Fe3+) ions were compared and screened respectively, and the POD-like activity of the prepared CDs were evaluated in the presence of H2O2 using TMB as the substrate. When CDs (0.04 mg/mL), TMB (0.2 mM) and H2O2 (1.25 mM) doped with different metals were incubated at 30℃ and pH= 5.0 for 15 min, the sequence of POD-like activity was as follows: Cu, S-CDs> > Ce4+, S-CDs> Ce3+, S-CDs>Fe3+, S-CDs, while the POD-like enzyme activity of CDs prepared with other metals was negligible (Fig. S5). These results indicated that Cu, S-CDs had higher POD-like enzyme activity. Through the Cu 2p spectrum of Cu, S-CDs (Fig. 2f), it had also been calculated that Cu+: Cu2+ was 1.74:1, which indicated that higher Cu+ content featured Cu, S-CDs higher Fenton-like reactivity under weakly acidic and neutral media, and ultimately higher POD-like activity [12].

Moreover, in order to better clarify the roles of ligands in the POD-like activity, the other two ligands with different coordination groups and heteroatoms were chosen as precursors to prepare Cu, N-CDs and Cu, N, S-CDs, which were prepared from CuCl2 and 2, 2′- (azo-1, 2-diyl) dibenzoic acid, bis (2-aminophenyl) disulfide respectively (Fig. 4b). It could be seen from Fig. 4c and Fig. 4d that there was POD-like activity and almost no oxidase-like activity of Cu, S-CDs, Cu, N-CDs and Cu, N, S-CDs when TMB were also employed as typical chromogenic substrates in the presence of H2O2. Remarkably, the reason why these three CDs exhibited POD-like activity was that S or N vacancies in CDs could mediate electron transfer between the HOMO of TMB and the LUMO of H2O2, thus promoting H2O2 to generate •OH and oxidize TMB.

Besides, through further analysis of the POD-like activities of them, it was found that the POD-like activity of Cu, S-CDs was 3.52-flod than that of Cu, N-CDs, because the S atom in Cu, S-CDs had a higher thermodynamic affinity than the N atom in Cu, N-CDs and could rapidly bind with Cu2+. As the affinity with Cu2+ was enhanced, the charge transfer of excited state to Cu2+ was promoted so that Cu, S-CDs had better enzyme-like activity. While, by contrast, the POD-like activity of Cu, S-CDs was 8.60 times than that of Cu, N, S-CDs, which indicated that Cu, S-CDs had good POD-like activity due to the synergistic effect of the carboxyl group of DSTA with the coordination structure of Cu2+ [50]. To sum up, all these pointed to the fact that the binding ability of S atom to Cu2+ and the synergistic coordinated interaction of carboxyl group to Cu2+ had significant effects on the nanozyme activities of Cu, S-CDs.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Optimization of the Pictet-Spengler condensation for the formation of fluorescent products catalyzed by Cu, S-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: Inspired by the chemical reactions from Michael addition reaction and Pictet-Spengler reaction [33,34] under the oxidizing conditions, we then optimized phenolic substances to achieve the azocines heterocyclic structures on account of the POD-like activity of Cu, S-CDs. Moreover, synthesis, fabrication, or the reform for large Stokes shift, high QY and remarkable water solubility [51] materials usually utilize coumarin as a fluorophore in fluorescence sensors. So, DMB, one of coumarins, was selected as a candidate phenolic compound, and was condensed with DA under the catalysis of Cu, S-CDs to afford the fluorescent azocine substance with high QY and long fluorescence lifetime.

Meanwhile, other three representative phenolic substances (reported resorcinol [33–36], 2-naphthol [40] and another new 1-naphthol) were also selected as references for the comparative analysis under the same reactive concentrations with DA triggered by Cu, S-CDs and H2O2 ( Fig. 5a).

Results showed that new UV–vis characteristic absorption peaks appeared respectively at 401 nm, 415 nm, 410 nm and 438 nm in four reactive systems after 30 min (Fig. 5b). While, their fluorescence emissions appeared respectively at 483 nm, 512 nm, 488 nm, and 493 nm (Fig. 5c). It was worth mentioning that the fluorescence intensity of the DMB system was 5.74 times than that of resorcinol, 9.84 times than that of 1-naphthol and 6.64 times than that of 2-naphthol. Noticeably, the fluorescence color changes from blue to green of the four systems could be observed on the Commission Internationale de I 'eclairage (CIE) chromaticity diagram (Fig. 5d). These distinct chemical reactions and optical properties based on DMB and DA by Cu, S-CDs and H2O2 provided the foundation for applications in recognizing and detecting NO2 - indirectly across the comparative difference of fluorescent intensity.

The reaction equation between DA and DMB to generate azocine derivative was displayed in Fig. 6a. The aqueous solutions of azocine derivative had a distinct UV absorption peak at 438 nm and the fluorescence emission at 493 nm (Fig. 6b), and showed out yellow color at sun light, and strong green color at ultraviolet lamp (365 nm). Theoretical calculations showed that the highest occupied molecular orbital (HOMO) of the azocine derivative was located in the entire molecule, while the lowest empty orbital (LUMO) was located in the nitrogen heterocyclic part, indicating that the emission peak at 493 nm was attributable to the charge transfer between HOMO and LUMO levels in the natural transition orbital (Fig. 6c) [38].

Using Rhodamine 6B as the standard, the QY of azocine derivative in water at 450 nm excitation wavelength was 82.6 %. In addition, the fluorescence lifetime and fluorescence stability of azocine derivative were also measured. According to the experimental results (Fig. 6d), the average fluorescence lifetime was 3.57 ns. Finally, experiments demonstrated that the fluorescence intensity of azocine derivative did not change significantly under the long-term irradiation of ultraviolet lamps (365 nm), showing remarkable fluorescence stability (Fig. 6e), which provided powerful support for its fluorescence stability in practical applications. In addition, to further eliminate interference, it was experimentally proved that the self-fluorescence of the Cu, S-CDs in the detection system did not interfere with the detection of NO2 - because its fluorescence was negligible compared with that of azocine derivative when the excitation wavelength was 450 nm (Fig. S3d).

The fluorescence spectra of reaction systems at different reaction times were measured at 30℃ with the Pictet-Spengler reaction of DA and DMB. As could be seen from the results in Fig. 7a, with the extension of reaction time, the fluorescent intensity of system at 493 nm gradually increased, reached a peak at 22 min, and then remained stable (Fig. 7b), indicating that the reaction was finished within 22 min.

In addition, the effects of different concentrations of DA on the fluorescent intensity of the system catalyzed by Cu, S-CDs were also studied. As shown in Fig. 7c, with the increase of DA concentrations, the fluorescence intensity ratio (F/F0) displayed continuous increase. And there was a strong linear relationship (F/F0=1.65x+0.99, R
2 =0.9991) between the fluorescence intensity ratio (F/F0) and DA concentrations in a range of 0–105 μM (Fig. 7d), which laid the foundation for the subsequent detection of NO2 -.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Optimization of DA-mediated fluorescence detecting conditions to NO2 - catalyzed by Cu, S-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: The ethanol content and pH were important parameters during NO2 - detection using DA as substrate. As could be seen from Fig. S6a, the fluorescent intensities of the system gradually decreased with the increase of EtOH contents. When H2O: EtOH= 4:1, the fluorescence emission intensity was basically stable, which indicated that the deamination reaction of DA and NO2 - was basically completed under this condition.

Subsequently, the influence of pH value on recognition performance of NO2 - in fluorescence was further explored. As presented in Fig. S6b, the fluorescent intensities of the system hardly changed at pH= 5.0 and 6.0, gradually decreased with the decrease of pH value and was the lowest as pH= 2.0. It was because the acidity affected the diazotization reaction progress. Thence, pH= 2.0 and H2O: EtOH= 4: 1 were selected as the optimal conditions for NO2 - to react with DA.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Study on DA-mediated fluorescence detection to NO2 -catalyzed by Cu, S-CDs
ce:section.ce:para:
The fluorescent titration of NO2 - was performed for the sensitivity evaluation, and results showed that the fluorescent intensities of the system were gradually trending downward with the increased NO2 - concentrations from 0 to 210 μM ( Fig. 8a). Fluorescent titration results showed that fluorescence intensity ratio (F/F0) of the system was linearly correlated with the concentrations of NO2 - in the range of 0.076–90 μM. The linear equation was F/F0= -0.0074x+ 0.99, R
2 was 0.9971 (Fig. 8b), and LOD was 0.062 μM (3σ/slope), which meant that the above detection assay was quite suitable for quantitatively detecting low concentrations of NO2 -.

To allow for a proper standpoint for differing NO2 - detection methods and their analytical results by nanozymes, a brief comparison had been made with some of the recent reports regarding the detection of NO2 - in Table S1.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Mechanism of DA-mediated fluorescence detection to NO2 - catalyzed by Cu, S-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: Traditional single-pathway-signal sensors show a variety of imperfection, such as the poor signal specificity and sensitivity, and the biggest challenge of these probes are their absolute intensity-dependent signal readout. Especially, as the detected substrate can not produce a strong fluorescence signal with the fluorescence detection probe, the substrate will not be recognized or detected by the fluorescence assay, not to mention reducing environmental interference during fluorescence detection by using the long-wavelength detection or high quantum efficiency of the probe. Therefore, to overcome the above shortcomings is critical, as this enables us to exercise independent control of fluorescence intensity, and ultimately exploit the signal to lead more reliable and sensitive sensing.

From the above, the indirect recognition and quantitative analysis of NO2 - by our strategy had been realized with comparative difference of fluorescent intensity (Scheme 1), of which DMB were used as reactants with DA to provide specific fluorescent substances through the fluorogenic reaction of Michael and Pictet-Spengler reaction, and DA-mediated fluorescent system coupled with the catalysis of Cu, S-CDs for detecting NO2 - was carried out as shown in Fig. 9. The experiment results further demonstrated that Cu, S-CDs had POD-like activities due to the high Fenton-like reactivity of Cu2+/Cu+ under weak acidity and neutral conditions, which could catalyze the reaction between DA and DMB well.

In addition, according to the mechanism of Michael and Pictet-Spengler reactions, DA and DMB were converted to the strong fluorescent azocine derivative, which was schematically as shown in Fig. 9a, and had QY of 82.6 %, fluorescence lifetime of 3.57 ns and good photostability.

Concretely, the elaborate reaction process supposedly indicated that, when Cu, S-CDs initiated H2O2 to generate •OH, DA was rapidly oxidized by •OH to an unstable o-quinone structure A, which could form an intermediate B through the intermolecular Michael addition reaction with DMB. Subsequently, the re-aromatization of intermediate B gave rise to product C, which was subsequently and rapidly oxidized to o-quinone D. After that, the intramolecular Michael addition reaction was triggered so that D was transformed into intermediate E. At last, azocine derivative were generated by rearrangement and intramolecular cyclization, which was verified by high-resolution mass spectrometry (Fig. S7).

Furthermore, Fig. 9b revealed the mechanism of DA consumption by NO2 - via deamination reaction [52,53] in existence of NO2 -. It was precisely because DA was consumed by NO2 - to produce 4-ethylcatechol that reduced the amount of DA in the system for detecting NO2 -, thus reducing or even completely blocking the amount of fluorescent azocine derivative accomplished from DA and DMB. The favorable linear quantitative relationship between NO2 - and fluorescence was obtained through the difference of fluorescent intensities of azocine derivative in the presence and absence of NO2 -. Accordingly, the concentration of NO2 - could be sensitively detected and realized with the comparative difference of fluorescent intensity. Characterizations by 1H NMR spectroscopy, 13C NMR spectroscopy, and high-resolution mass spectrometry confirmed the chemical structure of 4-ethylcatechol (Fig. S8-S10).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Study on selectivity and anti-interference of detecting NO2 - by Cu, S-CDs in fluorescence
ce:section.ce:para: In order to verify the specificity and selectivity of the DA-mediated fluorescence detection system catalyzed by Cu, S-CDs for the NO2 - response, a variety of interfering substances, including F-, Cl-, Br-, I-, SO4 2-, CO3 2-, NO3 -, H2PO4 -, ClO-, MnO4 -, Na+, K+, Ca2+, Mg2+, Zn2+ and Al3+, phenylalanine (Phe), glutamate (Glu), glycine (Gly), tyrosine (Tyr), vitamin C (Vc), glutathione (GSH) and 1-iodobutane, were studied. As displayed in Fig. S11, most of the above interfering ions could not influence the fluorescence of system with or without NO2 -, confirming the good performance of this proposed strategy in selective detection of NO2 -. Unfortunately, strong oxidizing substances and reducing substances would affect the NO2 - detection due to the oxidation of NO2 -, self-polymerization of DA or the reaction of DA with DMB. However, this problem could be overcome with targeted use of masking agents or inhibitor, such as ClO-- ammonia gas, MnO4 --SO3 2-, GSH-buthionine sulfoximine and Vc-ascorbic acid oxidase.

In addition, to check the interference-resistant ability of this sensing system, the above interfering substances were added, and experiments revealed that they did not affect the detection results of NO2 -. Thence, this system exhibited excellent selectivity and anti-interference ability in the detection of NO2 -.

ce:section.ce:section-title: Fluorescence detection of NO2 - in real samples by Cu, S-CDs
ce:section.ce:para: NO2 - in real samples was measured by the innovative DA-mediated system catalyzed by Cu, S-CDs with tap water, sausage and pickles as samples in the standard addition method. Subsequently, the content of NO2 - was determined by the match of the fluorescent intensity and linear equation (F/F0=-0.0074x+0.99), and the experimental results were shown in Table S2. The recoveries of NO2 - analysis were 98.3 %-104.0 %, and RSD (n = 3) were all less than 3.18 %. Moreover, the accuracy and reliability of the presented way for detecting NO2 - in practical samples was also validated by contrast with the spectrophotometric method again (Table S2) [54,55], which was the current Chinese national standards for NO2 - detection of spectrophotometric methods (GB 5009.33) [55,56]. Comparison results with the above standard methods showed that the assay now had some redeeming qualities in sensitivity, and detection time (Table S3).

ce:section.ce:section-title: Conclusion
ce:section.ce:para: In this study, a novel type of Cu-doped S-rich CDs nanozyme (Cu, S-CDs) with POD-like activity was successfully designed and synthesized. The results showed that the prepared Cu, S-CDs had intrinsic POD-like activity, and the catalytic mechanism was investigated in detail by EPR. Based on its catalytic effect on Pictet-Spengler reaction of different phenolic compounds and DA, it was determined that coumarin compound DMB reacted with DA to afford green fluorescent azocine derivative with high fluorescence QY. Finally, DA-mediated fluorescence system in high-performance detection for NO2 - was established, which could also be utilized for the accurate and visual determination of NO2 - in food and environment. Accordingly, this study provided reasonable ideas for the green synthesis of novel carbon-based nanozyme and the design of innovative catalytic oxidation pathways, and demonstrated latent potential in catalytic analysis and environmental detection of NO2 -.

ce:biography.ce:simple-para: Xiaohui Hao obtained her bachelor of engineering from Central South University and doctor's degree in University of Science and Technology Beijing. Her current research interest is analytical chemistry.

Ruochen Duan obtained his bachelor of engineering from Nanjing Agricultural University in 2018. Now he is studying his doctor's degree in University of Science and Technology Beijing. His current research interest is analytical chemistry.

Ao Shen obtained his doctor's degree from University of Science and Technology Beijing in 2023. Now he is lecturer in Henan University of Chinese Medicine. His current research interest is analytical chemistry.

Panqing Zhang obtained his master's degree from Zhengzhou University in 2022. Now he is studying his doctor's degree in University of Science and Technology Beijing. His current research interest is analytical chemistry.

Zeyu Zhang obtained his master's degree from North University of China in 2023. Now he is studying his doctor's degree in University of Science and Technology Beijing. His current research interest is analytical chemistry.

Xiuqing Zhao obtained her bachelor's degree in chemistry from Shandong University of Science and Technology in 2021 and her master degree in University of Science and Technology Beijing in 2024. Her current research interest is analytical chemistry.

Xuebing Wang obtained his bachelor's degree in chemistry from Taiyuan Normal University in 2021. Now he is studying his doctor's degree in University of Science and Technology Beijing. His current research interest is analytical chemistry.

Xue Li obtained her bachelor's degree in chemistry from Liaocheng University in 2021 and her master's degree in University of Science and Technology Beijing in 2024. Her current research interest is analytical chemistry.

Yunxu Yang obtained his doctor's degree in organic chemistry from Technical Institute of Physics and Chemistry, Chinese Academy of Sciences in 2005. Now he is a Professor in University of Science and Technology Beijing major in analytical chemistry. His current research interests are molecular recognition, supramolecular chemistry.
